families.note: Typed London mulligan, keep, and ordered bottom options are live; sealed benchmark fixtures remain absent.

Every live human and AI priority window now uses a shared canonical context with a forced-pass fast path and exact normal-cost instant targets, modes, optional answers, and payments. Non-mana activations, alternate costs, and resolution-time choices remain open.

Human and AI consume the same canonical land, mana activation, normal-cost permanent/instant/sorcery cast, and finish options. Cast descriptors bind exact targets, modes, optional answers, and every legal payment. Non-mana activations, variable/additional/alternate costs, and resolution-time object choices remain open.

Human and AI consume the same capped canonical Cartesian product assigning each legal attacker to no attack or any legal live player defender, including split multiplayer attacks; non-player defenders and sealed benchmark fixtures remain open.

Human and AI consume complete capped canonical blocker combinations for every attacked player in deterministic APNAP order, with declarations accumulated by the kernel; strategic damage ordering and sealed benchmark fixtures remain open.
The live human and AI paths enumerate, validate, dispatch, replay, and record both canonical move-or-leave options; sealed benchmark fixtures remain absent.

Normal spell casts enumerate the exact legal target Cartesian product and preserve selected targets through stack resolution. Activated abilities, target distribution, and partial-illegality resolution remain open.
Normal modal spell branches are canonical, visible to humans, available to AI, hashed on the stack, and bound at resolution. Modal activated and triggered abilities remain open.
Typed IDs exist; no complete production prompt adapter is claimed.
Current main-phase options bind explicit mana plans; general strategic payment prompts remain open.

Normal spell optional-effect products are canonical, visible to humans, available to AI, hashed on the stack, and bound at resolution. Optional costs and activated/triggered/resolution-time optional prompts remain open.
The current diagnostics driver permits only autonomous triggers.
Typed hidden-slot IDs exist; teacher-corpus-safe production prompting remains open.
Interpreter choices exist in semantic probes; no complete live-game adapter is claimed.
Automatic legal damage is exercised, but strategic order and assignment prompts remain open.
